nodes.label: Strait of Hormuz Disruption Risk
nodes.description: 20% of global oil supply passes through Hormuz; conflict threatens closure or mining of strait.
nodes.confidence_note: Iran has historically threatened strait closure during regional conflicts; immediate price spike likely.

headline_matches.headline: Pete Hegseth on Strait of Hormuz: 'Don't need to worry about it'
*IRAN MULLING LIMITED NUMBER OF TANKERS TO PASS IN HORMUZ: CNN

nodes.label: US Defense Mobilization
nodes.description: Increased military deployments and weapons procurement to Gulf region and support for Israel.
nodes.confidence_note: Historical pattern shows defense contractors rally 15-25% during Middle East escalations.

nodes.label: Risk-Off Asset Rotation
nodes.description: Investors flee equities for safe-haven assets amid geopolitical uncertainty and war premium.
nodes.confidence_note: Gold and Treasuries historically spike 5-10% during acute Middle East crises.

nodes.label: Cybersecurity Threat Elevation
nodes.description: Iranian state-sponsored groups target US critical infrastructure, financial systems, and energy grid.
nodes.confidence_note: Iran's cyber capabilities ranked among top 5 globally; past attacks on Saudi Aramco demonstrate capability.

nodes.label: Maritime Insurance Premium Surge
nodes.description: War risk premiums spike for tankers and container ships transiting Middle East waterways.
nodes.confidence_note: Shipping rates increase but demand destruction offsets; insurance costs can triple during conflict.

headline_matches.headline: *IRAN MULLING LIMITED NUMBER OF TANKERS TO PASS IN HORMUZ: CNN

nodes.label: Natural Gas Export Acceleration
nodes.description: Europe seeks alternative energy sources; US LNG exports surge as substitute for Middle East supply.
nodes.confidence_note: European energy crisis creates structural demand for US LNG; existing export terminals operate at capacity.

headline_matches.headline: Everyone Invested In Europe Should See This Chart

nodes.label: Energy-Driven Inflation Spike
nodes.description: Oil at $100+ drives headline CPI higher, complicating Fed's easing cycle and rate policy.
nodes.confidence_note: Energy comprises 7% of CPI basket; $20 oil increase adds roughly 0.7% to annual inflation.

headline_matches.headline: Sagging GDP Growth Is A Gut Punch And Accelerating Inflation Is A Hard Right Hook
Fourth-quarter GDP revised down to just 0.7% growth; January core inflation was 3.1%

2020: “This is temporary.”
2021: “Things will normalize soon.”
2022: “Just be patient.”
2023: “The inflation is cooling.”
2024: “The economy is strong.”
2025: Gas is up. Rent is up. Groceries are up. Unemployment is rising. War is escalating. AI is replacing jobs.

nodes.label: Federal Reserve Rate Cut Delay
nodes.description: Fed postpones easing cycle as inflation expectations rise and energy shock threatens price stability.
nodes.confidence_note: Fed Chair Powell has indicated readiness to pause cuts if inflation reaccelerates materially.

headline_matches.headline: Markets' hopes for Fed interest rate cuts are rapidly fading away

nodes.label: Discretionary Spending Contraction
nodes.description: Higher gasoline prices reduce disposable income for middle and lower-income consumers.
nodes.confidence_note: Every $0.50 gas increase reduces annual consumer spending by ~$70B in other categories.

nodes.label: Aviation Sector Profitability Squeeze
nodes.description: Jet fuel costs spike while carriers face difficulty passing through full cost increases to customers.
nodes.confidence_note: Fuel represents 20-30% of airline operating costs; unhedged carriers face acute margin pressure.

nodes.label: US Dollar Safe-Haven Bid
nodes.description: Global investors seek dollar liquidity and US asset safety, driving DXY index higher.
nodes.confidence_note: Dollar typically appreciates 3-7% during acute geopolitical crises as reserve currency of choice.

nodes.label: EM Debt and Equity Outflows
nodes.description: Strong dollar and risk-off sentiment trigger capital flight from emerging markets, especially energy importers.
nodes.confidence_note: Emerging markets face dual headwinds of dollar debt servicing costs and higher energy import bills.

nodes.label: Volatility Index Explosion
nodes.description: S&P 500 options market prices in war premium; VIX potentially moves from 15 to 30+ range.
nodes.confidence_note: Geopolitical tail risks historically double VIX levels within 2-3 trading sessions of conflict outbreak.

nodes.label: Growth Stock Multiple Compression
nodes.description: Rising rates expectations and risk-off sentiment hit high-valuation technology stocks disproportionately.
nodes.confidence_note: High-duration assets underperform in rising rate environments; tech trades at 28x forward earnings vs 19x S&P.

nodes.label: Israeli Semiconductor Disruption
nodes.description: Conflict threatens Israeli chip manufacturing and R&D facilities critical to Intel, NVIDIA, and Apple supply chains.
nodes.confidence_note: Israel produces critical chipmaking equipment and hosts major Intel and Tower Semiconductor fabs.

nodes.label: SPR Drawdown Authorization
nodes.description: US government releases Strategic Petroleum Reserve barrels to stabilize markets and cap price increases.
nodes.confidence_note: SPR currently holds ~350M barrels; releases of 1M bpd can temporarily suppress prices by $5-10.

Analysis: The U.S. is running out of ways to get oil prices down. It is up to the military.

nodes.label: China Strategic Alignment Risk
nodes.description: China continues purchasing Iranian oil at discount, potentially undermining Western sanctions effectiveness.
nodes.confidence_note: China imports 10% of oil from Iran; geopolitical alignment complicates US-China relations further.

headline_matches.headline: Global oil prices end week above $100, as U.S. waiver on some Russian sanctions fails to quell supply concerns
Lobster buffet: China’s tech firms feast on OpenClaw as companies race to deploy AI agents

nodes.label: Agricultural Input Cost Surge
nodes.description: Natural gas-derived fertilizer prices spike, threatening food production costs and agricultural margins.
nodes.confidence_note: Natural gas represents 70-90% of ammonia production costs; producers benefit from price increases.

nodes.label: China Helium Export Restrictions
nodes.description: China restricts helium exports as geopolitical leverage during Iran conflict, creating supply shock since China controls ~30% of global helium production and Iran tensions heighten strategic resource competition.
nodes.confidence_note: High likelihood given China's track record of export controls on strategic materials during geopolitical tensions, with direct impact on semiconductor and medical imaging supply chains.

scenarios.label: Contained Regional Conflict
scenarios.market_impact: Oil spikes to $95-100 temporarily before settling back; VIX touches 25-28; defense and energy outperform 2-3 weeks then fade.

scenarios.label: Hormuz Strait Blockade
scenarios.market_impact: Oil surges to $120-140; global recession fears trigger 15-20% equity selloff; Fed forced to choose between inflation and growth.

scenarios.label: Rapid De-escalation
scenarios.market_impact: Relief rally in equities 5-8%; oil gives back war premium falling to $70-75; VIX collapses back to 13-15 range.

scenarios.label: Multi-Front Middle East War
scenarios.market_impact: Systemic crisis with oil at $150+, 25%+ equity drawdown, credit market stress; Fed emergency intervention required.

nodes.label: Semiconductor fab cooling costs surge
nodes.description: Helium is critical for cooling and purging in semiconductor fabrication, directly raising production costs and threatening fab uptime.
nodes.confidence_note: Helium is non-substitutable in many chipmaking steps, and any supply disruption directly threatens semiconductor yields and margins.

nodes.label: MRI scanner operations threatened
nodes.description: Liquid helium is the only coolant capable of maintaining superconducting MRI magnets at operational temperatures, putting hospital imaging capacity at risk.
nodes.confidence_note: MRI machines require continuous helium replenishment, and a shortage directly curtails diagnostic imaging capacity at hospitals globally.

nodes.label: Rocket propulsion and defense testing halted
nodes.description: Helium is used to pressurize rocket fuel tanks and purge systems; a shortage disrupts launch schedules and defense testing programs.
nodes.confidence_note: NASA and DOD programs rely on helium for launch operations, and supply constraints could delay critical national security payloads.

nodes.label: Fiber optic cable production constrained
nodes.description: Helium is used as a cooling gas in fiber optic cable manufacturing to rapidly cool glass fibers during drawing, slowing global broadband infrastructure build-out.
nodes.confidence_note: With global fiber rollout accelerating for 5G and AI data center connectivity, helium-constrained fiber production creates a critical bottleneck.

nodes.label: Helium producers capture price premium
nodes.description: The small set of companies producing and distributing helium benefit from dramatically higher spot prices during a shortage event.
nodes.confidence_note: Air Products and Linde control significant helium liquefaction capacity, and price spikes directly expand margins on their helium supply agreements.

nodes.label: AI GPU and logic chip supply tightens
nodes.description: Fab production cuts cascade into GPU and advanced logic chip shortages, directly impacting AI infrastructure buildout timelines.
nodes.confidence_note: With AI capex already straining chip supply, any additional fab disruption amplifies GPU scarcity and extends delivery lead times for hyperscalers.

nodes.label: Hyperscaler AI capex timelines slip
nodes.description: Chip scarcity forces Microsoft, Google, and Amazon to delay data center deployments, compressing near-term AI revenue ramp expectations.
nodes.confidence_note: Hyperscalers have guided to hundreds of billions in AI capex; any chip supply shock creates consensus earnings risk for the Magnificent 7.

nodes.label: Hospital imaging revenue declines sharply
nodes.description: Reduced MRI availability forces hospitals to defer high-margin imaging procedures, directly compressing revenue at health systems.
nodes.confidence_note: MRI is among the highest-margin outpatient procedures; reduced scanner availability directly impairs hospital EBITDA and volume guidance.

nodes.label: Helium-free MRI technology demand spikes
nodes.description: Hospitals accelerate adoption of zero-boil-off and helium-free MRI systems to eliminate supply chain vulnerability, benefiting next-gen equipment makers.
nodes.confidence_note: GE HealthCare's SIGNA Premier zero-helium-boil-off MRI systems see accelerated order pipelines as hospitals de-risk helium dependency.

nodes.label: Industrial gas substitution R&D accelerates
nodes.description: Higher helium prices incentivize chemical and gas companies to fast-track alternative coolant and purging solutions, reshaping the industrial gas competitive landscape.
nodes.confidence_note: While substitution is limited in many applications, pricing pressure creates R&D investment cycles that could structurally alter helium demand curves over 3-5 years.

nodes.label: Federal helium reserve policy emergency review
nodes.description: DOD and DOE trigger emergency review of the Federal Helium Reserve following the 2013 Helium Stewardship Act, potentially reinstituting government stockpiling mandates.
nodes.confidence_note: The US sold off its strategic helium reserve under Congressional mandate; a shortage triggers bipartisan pressure to restore federal stockpiling authority.

nodes.label: Supply shock feeds into core goods inflation
nodes.description: Helium-driven semiconductor and medical equipment cost increases pass through into core goods CPI, complicating the Fed's rate path and re-anchoring inflation expectations.
nodes.confidence_note: A supply-side inflation shock in semiconductors and medical devices directly feeds into PCE components the Fed watches for evidence of re-acceleration.

nodes.label: Fed holds rates higher for longer
nodes.description: Supply-side inflationary pressures prevent the Fed from cutting rates as quickly as markets price, repricing duration risk across fixed income and equities.
nodes.confidence_note: Higher-for-longer rates would compress tech multiples and increase refinancing risk for leveraged companies, amplifying equity volatility.

nodes.label: Volatility spike as growth narrative breaks
nodes.description: Repriced rate expectations combined with AI infrastructure delays trigger a sentiment shock, spiking VIX and forcing systematic de-risking by vol-targeting funds.
nodes.confidence_note: Vol-targeting and risk-parity funds mechanically sell equities when VIX rises, creating a self-reinforcing de-risking cycle that amplifies the initial shock.

nodes.label: Qatar and Russia leverage helium export control
nodes.description: Qatar (world's largest helium exporter) and Russia (second largest before sanctions) gain geopolitical leverage, prompting Western nations to accelerate domestic helium exploration.
nodes.confidence_note: Qatar supplies roughly 30% of global helium; any export restriction or pricing leverage mirrors the OPEC playbook and triggers Western energy security responses.

nodes.label: US and Canadian helium exploration stocks surge
nodes.description: Geopolitical helium risk drives capital toward domestic helium exploration plays in the US mid-continent and Canadian prairies, creating a speculative junior miner rally.
nodes.confidence_note: Junior helium explorers in Wyoming, Kansas, and Saskatchewan have seen equity re-ratings during prior helium shortages as scarcity premiums attract speculative capital.

nodes.label: Quantum computing timelines extend materially
nodes.description: Quantum computers require liquid helium to maintain near-absolute-zero qubit temperatures; a helium shortage directly delays quantum hardware scaling and commercialization.
nodes.confidence_note: Dilution refrigerators used in quantum computing consume significant liquid helium, and supply constraints could set back the quantum race by 2-4 years.

nodes.label: GPS and surveillance satellite launches slip
nodes.description: Helium shortages delay Falcon 9 and Vulcan Centaur launches, pushing back GPS III, classified NRO payloads, and allied defense satellite constellations.
nodes.confidence_note: SpaceX Falcon 9 uses helium for tank pressurization on every launch; a sustained shortage creates a bottleneck across the entire US launch manifest.

scenarios.label: Acute Global Helium Supply Shock
scenarios.market_impact: A Qatari export disruption or simultaneous Russian/US plant outage triggers a >200% spot price spike, causing widespread fab shutdowns, MRI moratoriums, and a VIX spike above 30 as AI infrastructure timelines collapse.

scenarios.label: Sustained Moderate Helium Tightness
scenarios.market_impact: Helium prices stay 40-80% above historical norms for 12-18 months, creating persistent margin headwinds for semiconductor fabs and medical device makers while industrial gas majors like LIN and APD outperform the market by 15-20%.

scenarios.label: Government Intervention Stabilizes Supply
scenarios.market_impact: Congress reinstates strategic helium reserve purchasing and the DOE fast-tracks domestic exploration permits, stabilizing prices within 6 months and limiting sector damage to a brief 5-10% drawdown in exposed tech names.

scenarios.label: Rapid Technology Substitution Offsets Shortage
scenarios.market_impact: Helium-free MRI adoption accelerates, chipmakers shift to nitrogen purging in non-critical steps, and quantum firms pivot to helium recycling systems, limiting macro impact to a short-lived spike and benefiting medical device innovators.
